name: Corn Bread II
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare a large cast iron skillet by greasing it generously with bacon drippings to ensure the bottom of the skillet is well covered.
2. Place the skillet in a preheated oven at 400°F.
3. Meanwhile, prepare the batter: Combine the dry ingredients (corn meal, baking powder, soda, flour, and salt) in a bowl. Add the eggs and buttermilk, mixing until just combined.
4. Remove the skillet from the oven when the grease starts to smoke. Scrape the batter into the skillet so it sizzles immediately upon contact with the hot surface.
5. Return the skillet to the oven and continue baking for 20-25 minutes, or until the corn bread is golden brown on top.
6. After baking, allow the corn bread to cool in the skillet before transferring it to a serving dish.
Note: For best results, use this skillet exclusively for cornbread. Wipe it clean after each use without washing it. With practice, the cornbread will become easy to release from the pan while remaining intact.
